message:
test(scheduler): harden runnable-work, collision, and fairness invariants

This commit substantially expands scheduler-oriented correctness coverage across the global injector, priority scheduler internals, and three-lane worker coordination. The new tests focus on edge conditions that can regress silently under refactors: runnable-work classification, dense/overflow collision handling, fairness boundaries, and deterministic dispatch behavior.

Global injector coverage now validates `has_runnable_work` semantics for empty, cancel, ready, and timed-only queues across not-due/exactly-due/past-due transitions, and verifies `peek_earliest_deadline` consistency after both direct timed pops and due-only pops. This makes deadline-visibility behavior explicit at queue boundaries.

Priority scheduler coverage now exercises ScheduledSet dense-collision transitions with same-index/different-generation TaskIds, collapse behavior when collisions shrink, dedup behavior while in collision mode, overflow routing for high indices, dense growth behavior, and collision-aware dispatch ordering. It also adds deterministic certificate matching across independent scheduler instances and validates half-steal invariants for `steal_ready_batch_into`.

Three-lane scheduler coverage adds non-power-of-two worker coordinator round-robin checks, power-of-two bitmask path checks, single-worker/zero-worker corner cases, explicit validation of default cancel streak fairness (`DEFAULT_CANCEL_STREAK_LIMIT`), and region quiescence behavior checks through RuntimeState task lifecycle execution. Together these lock in fairness and quiescence expectations under cancel-heavy pressure.

`src/test_logging.rs` is also adjusted for equivalent but clearer failure-class assignment ordering (`if passed` first), aligning with strict linting expectations while preserving behavior.
